deep_dive.title: Knowledge Distillation for Visual Autoregressive Models
deep_dive.abstract: Autoregressive (AR) image generation models are highly expressive but computationally intensive, motivating effective model compression. Knowledge distillation (KD) is a natural approach for model compression and has been widely studied in language modeling, yet its behavior in visual AR generation remains underexplored. In this work, we present the first systematic study of distillation strategies for AR image models. Our analysis shows that while standard distillation can yield meaningful gains, recent methods developed for language do not directly transfer to images: long decoding horizons and visual token ambiguity make teacher supervision unreliable especially under student-conditioned contexts. To address this, we propose VarKD, a distillation framework for visual autoregressive models that distills on student samples while selectively applying teacher supervision and reducing token-level ambiguity. Experiments on ImageNet across multiple AR backbones show that VarKD consistently outperforms prior distillation baselines, narrowing the gap to large-scale models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: knowledge distillation strategies for visual autoregressive image models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向视觉自回归图像模型的知识蒸馏策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向视觉自回归图像模型的知识蒸馏策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向视觉自回归图像模型的知识蒸馏策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: VarKD systematically studies distillation for visual autoregressive models and proposes a novel framework addressing token ambiguity and long decoding horizons, achieving effective model compression.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉自回归图像模型的计算密集型问题，首次系统研究了知识蒸馏策略，发现语言领域的蒸馏方法不能直接迁移。提出VarKD框架，通过处理视觉令牌歧义和长解码视野问题，实现了有效的模型压缩。该方法为视频压缩模型的知识蒸馏提供了可直接借鉴的策略，有望降低视频编解码模型的复杂度。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对视觉自回归图像模型的计算密集型问题，首次系统研究了知识蒸馏策略，发现语言领域的蒸馏方法不能直接迁移。提出VarKD框架，通过处理视觉令牌歧义和长解码视野问题，实现了有效的模型压缩。该方法为视频压缩模型的知识蒸馏提供了可直接借鉴的策略，有望降低视频编解码模型的复杂度。

quick_skim.title: PivCo-Huffman
quick_skim.abstract: Huffman encoding has been an enduring technique for 70+ years, ubiquitous in compression algorithms since its invention. In this paper we propose a new approach to Huffman coding, based on a data structure from wavelet trees. The resulting pivot-coded Huffman (PivCo-Huffman) enables high-performance SIMD-friendly encoding and decoding operations. In our tests PivCo-Huffman consistently outperforms state-of-the-art Huffman codecs in decoding throughput. Additionally, we show how ANS-coding can be selectively applied to skewed nodes in this structure, yielding compression ratios approaching those of ANS-based codecs while preserving very high decompression speeds.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a new Huffman codec with SIMD-friendly operations, optimizing entropy coding
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出了一种新的哈夫曼编解码器，具有SIMD友好操作，优化了熵编码
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出了一种新的哈夫曼编解码器，具有SIMD友好操作，优化了熵编码
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出了一种新的哈夫曼编解码器，具有SIMD友好操作，优化了熵编码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PivCo-Huffman introduces a pivot-coded Huffman encoding based on wavelet trees, achieving higher decoding throughput and competitive compression ratios compared to ANS.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有哈夫曼编码在解码吞吐量上存在瓶颈，且与ANS等先进编码器相比压缩率有差距。本文提出PivCo-Huffman，基于小波树数据结构实现枢轴编码的哈夫曼编码，支持SIMD友好的高速编解码操作。实验表明，PivCo-Huffman在解码吞吐量上持续优于最先进的哈夫曼编解码器，并通过选择性应用ANS编码，在保持极快解压速度的同时逼近ANS的压缩率。该方法为熵编码模块的优化提供了新的方向，可直接集成到视频编解码框架中。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有哈夫曼编码在解码吞吐量上存在瓶颈，且与ANS等先进编码器相比压缩率有差距。本文提出PivCo-Huffman，基于小波树数据结构实现枢轴编码的哈夫曼编码，支持SIMD友好的高速编解码操作。实验表明，PivCo-Huffman在解码吞吐量上持续优于最先进的哈夫曼编解码器，并通过选择性应用ANS编码，在保持极快解压速度的同时逼近ANS的压缩率。该方法为熵编码模块的优化提供了新的方向，可直接集成到视频编解码框架中。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: optimization of entropy coding modules in deep video compression

quick_skim.title: SwiftVR: Real-Time One-Step Generative Video Restoration
quick_skim.abstract: Real-time video restoration (VR) for live streams requires high-resolution outputs under strict per-frame latency constraints. Existing one-step diffusion-based VR models remain difficult to deploy on consumer-grade GPUs due to two main bottlenecks: quadratic spatial attention at high resolutions and the latency-memory overhead of large video autoencoders. We present SwiftVR, a streaming one-step generative VR framework that reduces both bottlenecks under a causal chunk-wise protocol. For attention, mask-free shifted-window self-attention gathers each spatial window into a dense tensor via deterministic indexing, keeping all attention calls on the dense scaled dot-product attention path without masks, cyclic shifts, padding, or hardware-specific sparse kernels. Because SwiftVR uses only standard dense SDPA calls, the trained model transfers to consumer GPUs without retraining or custom kernels. For autoencoding, a lightweight Restoration-aware Autoencoder enables fast chunk-wise decoding while preserving reconstruction quality. On a single H100, SwiftVR sustains 31~FPS at 2560x1440 and 14~FPS at 3840x2160, whereas all compared diffusion-based VR baselines exceed the memory limit at 4K. On a consumer RTX~5090, SwiftVR reaches 26~FPS at 1920x1080. To our knowledge, SwiftVR is the first generative VR model to achieve real-time 1080p streaming on a consumer-grade GPU, while attaining strong no-reference perceptual quality with lower inference cost. Project is available at https://h-oliday.github.io/SwiftVR.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: generative video restoration using diffusion, related to generative video compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用扩散模型的生成式视频恢复，与生成式视频压缩相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用扩散模型的生成式视频恢复，与生成式视频压缩相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用扩散模型的生成式视频恢复，与生成式视频压缩相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SwiftVR proposes a real-time one-step generative video restoration framework using mask-free shifted-window self-attention and causal chunk-wise processing, reducing latency and memory.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: SwiftVR提出了一种实时一步生成式视频恢复框架，通过无掩码移位窗口自注意力和因果分块处理，降低了延迟和内存消耗，适用于直播等实时场景。该框架基于扩散模型，通过高效的注意力机制和轻量级自编码器实现了在消费级GPU上的部署，为生成式视频压缩提供了可借鉴的快速推理思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: SwiftVR提出了一种实时一步生成式视频恢复框架，通过无掩码移位窗口自注意力和因果分块处理，降低了延迟和内存消耗，适用于直播等实时场景。该框架基于扩散模型，通过高效的注意力机制和轻量级自编码器实现了在消费级GPU上的部署，为生成式视频压缩提供了可借鉴的快速推理思路。

quick_skim.title: Joint Structural Pruning and Mixed-Precision Quantization for LLM Compression
quick_skim.abstract: Recently, the efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs) deployment has become a critical concern in practical applications. While post-training quantization (PTQ) and structural pruning are established techniques for reducing memory footprint and inference latency, most existing PTQ approaches optimize quantization errors on a per-layer basis, overlooking how errors accumulate and propagate through the network, often resulting in suboptimal solutions. Traditional pipelines also tend to apply pruning and quantization in isolation or sequentially, further compounding sub-optimality. We introduce a novel end-to-end framework that addresses these limitations in two key ways. First, we propose a novel mixed-precision PTQ strategy that directly minimizes global error propagation across the entire model, rather than isolating layer-wise errors. Building on this, we develop a novel joint optimization approach that simultaneously learns structural pruning decisions and mixed-precision quantization policies within a unified search space. Extensive experiments show that, at ultra-low precisions (1-3 bits), our quantization method reduces WikiText perplexity by up to 21% compared to state-of-the-art (SoTA) weight-activation quantization baselines. Against leading weight-only quantization methods, it achieves up to 59% and 85% lower perplexity on WikiText and C4, respectively. Compared to the SoTA joint pruning-and-quantization techniques, our proposed method delivers superior perplexity and reasoning performance at ultra-low bits.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: joint pruning and quantization for compression, transferable to video codec training tricks
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 联合剪枝与量化用于压缩，可迁移至视频编解码训练技巧
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 联合剪枝与量化用于压缩，可迁移至视频编解码训练技巧
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 联合剪枝与量化用于压缩，可迁移至视频编解码训练技巧
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an end-to-end framework for joint structural pruning and mixed-precision quantization of LLMs, directly minimizing error propagation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种端到端框架，联合优化大语言模型的结构剪枝和混合精度量化，直接最小化误差累积和传播。与视频压缩中的模型轻量化（剪枝、量化）需求高度相关，其联合优化思路可迁移至神经视频编解码的训练技巧，如量化感知训练和模型加速。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种端到端框架，联合优化大语言模型的结构剪枝和混合精度量化，直接最小化误差累积和传播。与视频压缩中的模型轻量化（剪枝、量化）需求高度相关，其联合优化思路可迁移至神经视频编解码的训练技巧，如量化感知训练和模型加速。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索视频压缩模型的训练 trick，包括量化、蒸馏、剪枝、多阶段训练等技术，调研不同优化策略对编码性能、推理速度、模型大小的提升效果，设计适用于端侧部署的轻量化训练方案。, especially work that connects or combines: Low-complexity codec; neural video compression model training techniques; quantization aware training for video coding models; knowledge distillation strategies for video compression; model pruning methods for lightweight video codecs; multi-stage training pipeline for neural video compression; Differentiable quantization optimization for latent in video compression; Knowledge distillation for efficient neural video codec training; Model pruning and acceleration for end to end video coding; Multi stage training strategy for rate distortion performance improvement.

quick_skim.title: NSVQ: Mitigating Codebook Collapse by Stabilizing Encoder Drift in Vector Quantization
quick_skim.abstract: Vector quantization is central to modern generative modeling pipelines, but large-codebook VQ models often suffer from codebook collapse. We identify encoder drift as a key driver of this failure: as the encoder moves the latent distribution, sparsely updated code vectors can lag behind, lose assignments, and increase quantization error, creating a feedback loop through the straight-through estimator. We propose NSVQ, a non-stationary-aware VQ training strategy that combines a dense non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and stage-wise encoder freezing. NSVQ first helps the codebook track encoder drift during early training, then freezes the encoder to consolidate the codebook under a fixed latent geometry, and finally reintroduces adversarial refinement. Experiments on ImageNet-1k show that NSVQ improves reconstruction quality while maintaining full codebook utilization. On ImageNet-1k at 128$\times$128 with 65,536 codes, NSVQ reduces rFID from 2.39 to 2.10 compared with SimVQ, while both methods maintain 100\% utilization. Additional latent diffusion experiments show that NSVQ also improves downstream ImageNet generation FID.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: non-stationary-aware VQ training strategy for codebook collapse
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对码本崩溃的非平稳感知VQ训练策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对码本崩溃的非平稳感知VQ训练策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对码本崩溃的非平稳感知VQ训练策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: NSVQ mitigates codebook collapse in vector quantization by stabilizing encoder drift with a dense non-stationary embedding loss, codebook replacement, and stage-wise encoder freezing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 向量量化中，编码器漂移导致码本崩溃。本文提出NSVQ，采用非平稳嵌入损失、码本替换和阶段式冻结编码器，使码本在训练早期跟踪潜在分布变化，后期稳定几何结构。实验有效减少码本崩溃，提升重建质量，可用于视频编码中的量化模块优化。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 向量量化中，编码器漂移导致码本崩溃。本文提出NSVQ，采用非平稳嵌入损失、码本替换和阶段式冻结编码器，使码本在训练早期跟踪潜在分布变化，后期稳定几何结构。实验有效减少码本崩溃，提升重建质量，可用于视频编码中的量化模块优化。

quick_skim.title: Sigma-Branch: Hierarchical Single-Path Network Reconstruction for Dynamic Inference with Reduced Active Parameters
quick_skim.abstract: Deploying deep neural networks on memory-constrained edge accelerators is bottlenecked by per-inference off-chip weight transfer rather than computation: the dense network cannot be retained on-chip, and every parameter must be loaded for every input. Existing model compression reduces this transfer only at the cost of permanent capacity loss. We propose Sigma-Branch (SigmaB), a framework that restructures a pretrained dense network into a hierarchical binary tree composed of a shared backbone, hierarchical routers, and specialized leaves. Pretrained weights are distributed across the tree via activation-based spherical k-means clustering, which jointly initializes router weights and per-branch channel allocations; soft-routing fine-tuning then aligns each leaf with its routed input subset. At inference, the resulting network executes only a single root-to-leaf path, reducing the active-parameter footprint while storing the complete dense parameter set in memory. Across CIFAR-100 / ResNet-50, ImageNet-1K / ResNet-50, and ModelNet40 / PointNet++, SigmaB-Net reduces per-inference active parameters by 58-60% while remaining within 1.72 percentage points (pp) of the dense baseline Top-1. At comparable ImageNet-1K Top-1, the active-parameter reduction exceeds static structured pruning (FPGM, HRank) by 14-23 pp. The cross-modal evaluation, spanning 2D vision and 3D point-cloud backbones, substantiates a framework-level claim that decouples per-inference memory traffic from the total parameter count.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: model compression via hierarchical reconstruction and dynamic inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过层次化重构和动态推理实现模型压缩
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过层次化重构和动态推理实现模型压缩
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过层次化重构和动态推理实现模型压缩
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Sigma-Branch to restructure dense networks into a hierarchical binary tree, reducing active parameters for edge deployment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对边缘设备上深度神经网络每次推理需传输全部权重的问题，提出Sigma-Branch框架，将密集网络重构为层次化二叉树结构，包含共享主干、层次路由器和专用叶子节点。通过基于激活的球形k均值聚类分配预训练权重，在保持模型容量的同时显著减少活跃参数数量，适用于低复杂度视频编解码器的设计。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对边缘设备上深度神经网络每次推理需传输全部权重的问题，提出Sigma-Branch框架，将密集网络重构为层次化二叉树结构，包含共享主干、层次路由器和专用叶子节点。通过基于激活的球形k均值聚类分配预训练权重，在保持模型容量的同时显著减少活跃参数数量，适用于低复杂度视频编解码器的设计。

quick_skim.title: Benchmarking Neural Speech Compression from a Rate-Distortion Perspective
quick_skim.abstract: Learning-based speech compression has achieved promising low-bitrate performance, but many neural speech codecs still describe quantized latents with preset-rate discrete symbols or apply entropy coding only after symbol generation. Such designs decouple representation learning from probability modeling, limiting their ability to exploit the non-uniform usage and temporal dependencies of learned speech latents. In this paper, we benchmark neural speech compression from a rate--distortion perspective and further investigate entropy-constrained coding for low-bitrate speech compression. We first formulate a unified learning-based speech coding pipeline and provide a benchmark-style analysis of recent neural speech codecs, showing that explicit probability modeling remains underexplored in learned speech compression. We then propose ECC, an Entropy-Constrained Codec that combines scalar quantization with a learned entropy model. ECC integrates hyperprior-based side information, channel-wise context modeling, latent residual prediction, and lightweight temporal modeling to estimate latent likelihoods for rate estimation during training and arithmetic coding during inference. To further improve low-bitrate efficiency, ECC introduces entropy skip, which omits highly predictable residual symbols using decoder-available scale estimates without transmitting additional skip masks. Extensive experiments show that ECC achieves a favorable low-bitrate rate--distortion trade-off over conventional and neural codec baselines, reducing BD-rate by 39.9% on ViSQOL and 76.3% on PESQ on average over two widely-used test sets. Ablation and diagnostic studies further validate the effectiveness of entropy modeling. Project Page: https://avery-xu.github.io/ECC-demo/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: rate-distortion benchmarking and entropy-constrained coding for neural speech codecs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经语音编解码器的率失真基准测试及熵约束编码
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经语音编解码器的率失真基准测试及熵约束编码
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经语音编解码器的率失真基准测试及熵约束编码
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper benchmarks neural speech compression from a rate-distortion perspective, analyzes entropy coding limitations, and proposes entropy-constrained coding to improve low-bitrate performance, offering insights for video codecs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文从率失真角度对神经语音压缩进行了基准测试，揭示了现有神经语音编解码器在熵编码上表示学习与概率建模分离的问题，并提出熵约束编码方法以改善低比特率下的压缩性能。该工作为视频编解码的率失真优化提供了可迁移的分析框架和编码策略。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文从率失真角度对神经语音压缩进行了基准测试，揭示了现有神经语音编解码器在熵编码上表示学习与概率建模分离的问题，并提出熵约束编码方法以改善低比特率下的压缩性能。该工作为视频编解码的率失真优化提供了可迁移的分析框架和编码策略。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: rate-distortion optimization strategies for neural codecs
